title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Cr system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Cr binary; terminal fcc solubility + ordered phases

title: Okamoto et al., The Au-Co system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Co eutectic, large solid miscibility gap

title: J.F. Smith, The Au-V system
whatItEstablishes: Au-V; intermediate compounds, limited terminal solubility

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Sb system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Sb eutectic, AuSb2, very low Sb solubility

title: H. Okamoto, Au-In
whatItEstablishes: Au-In; intermetallics + appreciable terminal fcc solubility

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Te system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Te eutectic, AuTe2, negligible solubility

title: Massalski et al., The Au-Mo system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Mo near-immiscible, large miscibility gaps

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Nb system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Nb; Nb3Au (A15), limited solubility

title: J.L. Murray, The Au-Ti system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Ti; Au4Ti/AuTi intermetallics, modest solubility

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Hf system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Hf; intermetallics, restricted solubility

title: Massalski & Okamoto, The Au-Mn system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Mn extensive solid solubility + ordered phases

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-W system
whatItEstablishes: Au-W essentially immiscible, no compounds

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Re system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Re near-complete immiscibility

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Ta system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Ta limited solubility, Ta3Au-type compounds

title: H. Okamoto, Au-Zr
whatItEstablishes: Au-Zr intermetallics, modest terminal solubility

title: Okamoto et al., The Au-Cu system
whatItEstablishes: REFERENCE: complete fcc solution + AuCu/Au3Cu order

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Ag-Au system
whatItEstablishes: REFERENCE: complete fcc solubility (isomorphous)

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Pd system
whatItEstablishes: REFERENCE: complete fcc solubility (isomorphous)

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Si system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Si eutectic ~363C; near-zero mutual solubility

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Ge system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Ge eutectic ~361C; negligible solid solubility

title: H. Okamoto, Au-Ga
whatItEstablishes: Au-Ga intermetallics; limited terminal solubility

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Sn system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Sn (AuSn...AuSn4); Au-rich eutectic; solder system

title: Okamoto & Massalski, The Au-Bi system
whatItEstablishes: Au-Bi eutectic, Au2Bi; very low Bi solubility

title: H. Okamoto, Au-Nd
whatItEstablishes: Au-Nd intermetallics; negligible terminal solubility

title: H. Okamoto, Au-Pr
whatItEstablishes: Au-Pr line compounds; negligible solubility

title: Au Binary Alloy Phase Diagrams
whatItEstablishes: Consolidated Au-X binaries + crystallography; gap-filler for RE/Sr/Al/Sc

title: La Binary Alloy Phase Diagrams
whatItEstablishes: Au-La phase relations (line compounds, no terminal solubility)

title: Mae, What the Darken-Gurry Plot Means About Solubility
whatItEstablishes: Darken-Gurry ellipse predicts terminal solubility (size + electronegativity)

title: Miracle & Senkov, Critical Review of High Entropy Alloys
whatItEstablishes: Modern assessment of Hume-Rothery size/EN/VEC rules & limits

title: Pei et al., ML prediction of solid-solution formation
whatItEstablishes: ML predictors beyond classical Hume-Rothery rules

title: Elliott & Shunk, The Au-Ga system
whatItEstablishes: Earlier Au-Ga assessment (corroborates G21)

title: Multielement determination of used/unused lubricating oils by ICP-AES
whatItEstablishes: Primary ICP method; defines additive/wear/contaminant panel incl. Ti/V/Mn

title: Additive elements in lubricating oils by ICP-AES
whatItEstablishes: Additive-element panel (Mo measured natively = FM background)

title: Ca,Cl,Cu,Mg,P,S,Zn in oils by WDXRF
whatItEstablishes: WDXRF quantitation incl. S/P/Cl (P/S accounting)

title: Additive elements in oils by EDXRF
whatItEstablishes: EDXRF method - matches SMX XRF readout architecture

title: Ba,Ca,Mg,Zn in unused oils by AAS
whatItEstablishes: Working range ~10 ppm-1% confirms 5-50 ppm dosing detectable

title: Sulfated ash from lubricating oils and additives
whatItEstablishes: Defines sulfated ash (SA in SAPS) - core constraint for metal markers

title: Engine Oil Licensing & Certification System
whatItEstablishes: Explicitly permits performance-neutral markers; conformance audits

title: Base Oil Interchangeability Guidelines (Groups I-V)
whatItEstablishes: Base-oil group chemistry context (marker solubility, native S)

whatItEstablishes: GF-7/API SQ introduces 0.9% max sulfated ash (D874)

whatItEstablishes: Hydrocarbon-soluble Mo octoate (FM/AO) - heavy native Mo background

whatItEstablishes: MoDTC FM/MoS2 film; Mo-marker indistinguishable from FM Mo

title: Engine oil additive effects on TWC & O2 sensors
whatItEstablishes: Oil-derived P poisons TWC/O2 sensor; Ca/Mg mitigate - poisoning framework

title: Effects of oil phosphorus on TWC deactivation
whatItEstablishes: Quantifies P loss of washcoat area/OSC - poisoning reference

title: Deactivation of P-poisoned diesel oxidation catalysts
whatItEstablishes: Mechanistic P-poisoning of DOC - extends to diesel aftertreatment

whatItEstablishes: Sublimation pressures + stability for V and RE (Tm,Dy) acac

title: Three yttrium organometallics for YSZ by PE-CVD
whatItEstablishes: Y(TMHD)3 sublimes 92C; Y(acac)3 non-subliming (more retained)

title: Metallic soaps / oil drying agents (carboxylate solubility)

whatItEstablishes: Ceria fuel-borne catalyst transforms in combustion, persists in exhaust

title: MMT (methylcyclopentadienyl Mn tricarbonyl) review
whatItEstablishes: MMT -> Mn3O4 particulates degrade emission-control systems

title: Oil-soluble WS2 nanosheets & tribology
whatItEstablishes: W delivered as WS2/nanosheet (no simple W-carboxylate) - availability gap

title: Effect of TiO2 pigment type on UV degradation of coatings
whatItEstablishes: Anatase/uncoated TiO2 photodegrades binder; coated rutile is safe

whatItEstablishes: Oxide nanopowder melt-compounded into HDPE/PP (masterbatch route)

title: ZrO2 nanoparticles in polycarbonate

title: Nanofillers as thermal stabilizers in LDPE

title: Polyimide/Bi2O3 composites
whatItEstablishes: High Bi2O3 loadings raise composite thermal stability

whatItEstablishes: Bi2O3 well-tolerated, improves thermal stability in epoxy

title: Sb2O3/SnO2 in flexible PVC films
whatItEstablishes: Sb2O3/SnO2 oxides disperse & are processing-stable in PVC

whatItEstablishes: RE-oxide pigments: high thermal/chemical stability, good dispersibility

whatItEstablishes: RE-oxide colorants weather/UV-stable, high NIR reflectance

whatItEstablishes: Doped RE oxides thermally/chemically stable pigments

whatItEstablishes: Rutile TiO2 inert, processes ~280-300C without decomposition (corroborating)
